# @zakkster/lite-headless / descriptions

Key-value layout for admin detail views. Each item has a label + value;
items are arranged in a grid of 1–4 columns. Optionally bordered.

This is primarily a layout + ARIA primitive. The visual styling
(borders, padding, separator lines) is consumer-driven via CSS.

## Quick example

```js
import { createDescriptions } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/descriptions";

const d = createDescriptions({
    columns: 3,
    bordered: true,
});
d.attachRoot(rootEl);
d.attachItem(item1);   // each item has a [data-desc-label] + [data-desc-value]
d.attachItem(item2);
```

## Options

| Option | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `columns` | `1` | Grid column count (1–4) |
| `bordered` | `false` | Paints `data-bordered` for CSS borders |

## API

```ts
{
    columns: number      // readonly
    bordered: boolean    // readonly

    attachRoot(el): off
    attachItem(el): off

    destroy(): void
}
```

## Painted attributes

### Root (`[data-descriptions-root]`)

- `data-columns="<1|2|3|4>"`
- `data-bordered` — boolean, when `bordered: true`

### Items (`[data-desc-item]`)

- `role="group"` (only if not pre-set)

### Label + Value (consumer-authored slots)

- `[data-desc-label]` — gets an auto-generated `id`
- `[data-desc-value]` — gets `aria-labelledby="<label-id>"` for SR association

## Wrapper (`<lite-descriptions>`)

```html
<lite-descriptions columns="3" bordered>
    <div data-desc-item>
        <div data-desc-label>Username</div>
        <div data-desc-value>alice_dev</div>
    </div>
    <div data-desc-item>
        <div data-desc-label>Email</div>
        <div data-desc-value>alice@example.com</div>
    </div>
    <div data-desc-item>
        <div data-desc-label>Role</div>
        <div data-desc-value><lite-tag intent="primary">Admin</lite-tag></div>
    </div>
    <div data-desc-item>
        <div data-desc-label>Status</div>
        <div data-desc-value><lite-tag intent="success">Active</lite-tag></div>
    </div>
    <div data-desc-item>
        <div data-desc-label>Last login</div>
        <div data-desc-value>2 hours ago</div>
    </div>
    <div data-desc-item>
        <div data-desc-label>Member since</div>
        <div data-desc-value>March 2024</div>
    </div>
</lite-descriptions>
```

Attributes: `columns="1|2|3|4"`, `bordered`.

## CSS contract

```css
[data-descriptions-root] {
    display: grid;
    gap: 1px;
    background: var(--border-color);   /* shows through gap when bordered */
}
[data-descriptions-root][data-columns="1"] { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
[data-descriptions-root][data-columns="2"] { grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; }
[data-descriptions-root][data-columns="3"] { grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr; }
[data-descriptions-root][data-columns="4"] { grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; }

[data-desc-item] {
    background: var(--surface);
    padding: 12px 16px;
    display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px;
}
[data-desc-label] { font-size: 11px; opacity: 0.65; }
[data-desc-value] { font-size: 13px; }

/* Bordered: rely on the gap+background trick OR explicit borders */
[data-descriptions-root]:not([data-bordered]) {
    background: transparent;
}
```
